body: ### Bug description Compiling the below Rust code gives me a working binary, that I can then import as a module and run without any issues. However when I try to run pylint on the code that calls it pylint simply just hangs while trying to lint the binary. ```rust use pyo3::types::PyModuleMethods; #[pyo3::pyclass] #[pyo3(module = "happy_hour.refreshments")] #[derive(Clone, PartialEq)] pub enum Cocktails { Mojito { sugar: u32, rum: u32, mint: u32, lime_wedges: u32, soda_water: u32, consumers: Vec, }, MintJulep { bourbon: u32, mint: u32, consumers: Vec, }, } #[pyo3::pymodule] fn refreshments( py: pyo3::Python<'_>, m: &pyo3::Bound, ) -> pyo3::PyResult<()> { m.add_class::()?; return Ok(()); } ``` This essentially boils down to this: ```python class Cocktails: @final class Mojito(Cocktails): sugar: int rum: int mint: int lime_wedges: int soda_water: int consumers: list[int] @final class MintJulep(Cocktails): bourbon: int mint: int consumers: list[int] ``` ### Command used ```shell pylint module/xy.py ``` ### Pylint output pylint hangs on run. When I hook up a debugger I get it recursively calling `transforms._visit()` and `transforms._visit_generic()` ### Expected behavior Expecting the linting to finish without any issues ### Pylint version ```shell pylint 3.3.8 astroid 3.3.11 Python 3.12.10 ``` ### OS / Environment Windows 10

comments.body:
Thank you for opening the issue and for troubleshooting it already. 

Linting the resulting python file you gave (on ubuntu 24-04, pylint current main 4.0.0-dev0 / astroid 4.0.0a0, and python 3.12.3) takes 0.5s. I'm not sure I understood what is happening here but it seems pylint fails to parse what is generated from your rust code ? Any way you can let us compile this binary ourselves from rust so we can try to reproduce ?

Hello, @viktor-refeyn is on leave today so I'll fill in for him. Here is a simple setuptools-rust setup that reproduces the problem (note that the original Rust snippet has some syntax errors, I've corrected them in the zip):

[PYLINT-10513.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22042225/PYLINT-10513.zip)

title: Pylint hangs when trying to lint a pyo3 enum with named fields
